summary: Addresses greptile review #9 feedback: (1) adds generate-common Makefile target so internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go is regenerated with the SPDX header via controller-gen, (2) expands TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations to trigger cache and database mutual-exclusivity violations alongside all other validation branches, and (3) captures two review patterns from external feedback.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (make test OPERATOR=keystone exits 0)
Edge cases covered: cache/database mutual-exclusivity now exercised in RunsAllValidations
Assertions are specific (ContainSubstring for each distinct error field)
Test names match what they exercise

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Makefile target conventions (phony, @-prefixed, comment style)
Test follows Gomega pattern with NewGomegaWithT

security_checklist.item: No security-sensitive changes (build targets and test-only changes)

architecture_checklist.item: generate-common target correctly scoped to internal/common/types
generate depends on generate-common ensuring correct ordering
make generate && make manifests produce no diff (files in sync)

fail_fast_checklist.item: RunsAllValidations test proves error accumulation (6 distinct error substrings)

defensive_checklist.item: All validation branches tested individually in dedicated tests

suggested_improvements: Minor: TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations still does not trigger the maxActiveKeys<3 branch (validKeystone sets it to 3) or the empty-rule-name branch (empty PolicySpec has no Rules map). These are tested individually in TestValidate_MaxActiveKeysBelowMinimumRejected and TestValidate_EmptyPolicyRuleNameRejected, so the gap is cosmetic — but adding k.Spec.Fernet.MaxActiveKeys=1 and Rules with an empty key would make the name fully accurate.

metadata.title: E001: Add Keystone API types, webhooks & CRD generation
